filter_index.video_title: Why We Love Dayara Bugyal Trek | A Tribute by Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow present from base camp towards end of December
Upper reaches under snow until mid-April
No snow on the trek in autumn
Trek is closed/avoided during monsoons; trek is open for about 8 months in a year
temperature_notes: Winter: Day 8–12 °C, Night -5 to -10 °C; 5 warm layers required
Spring: Day 13–17 °C, Night drops to 0 °C; 3–4 warm layers required
Summer: Day 18–20 °C, Night drops to 5 °C; 3 warm layers required
Autumn: Day 12–15 °C, Night drops to 2 °C; 4 warm layers required

terrain_challenges: Initial climb into the forest from Raithal is steep
Final climb to Dayara top is steep
Daily ascents and descents, mostly gradual and steady except the summit climb on Day 4
Snow section near the top gets steeper as you approach the top
Snow deposits on the meadow are often several feet deep
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trail from Raithal to Gui climbs 2,478 ft within 4.5 km; trekkers should stay alert for signs of Acute Mountain Sickness
Above 10,000 ft, AMS risk is real for both first-time and experienced trekkers
Even fit trekkers can develop AMS if they climb too high too soon
Rainfall or snowfall lasting hours can change trek dynamics
Rain can flood trails or make them too slushy to walk safely

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog, or run 5 km in under 40 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Build cardiovascular endurance and muscle strength
Train core, calves, glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings
Walking, jogging, or running is the recommended training routine
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting the trek's fitness criteria
Runs must meet the trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month
prep_window: Upload fitness screenshots 15 to 20 days before the trek date
Runs must meet fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month

experience_themes: Winter: snow-covered landscape, powdery snowflakes on pine trees, possible snowfall, snowball fights
Spring: last of winter snow, melting snow in upper reaches, green grass in lower meadows, tiny flowers, blooming rhododendrons, colour contrasts of snow/greenery/flowers
Summer: bright green meadows, summer fragrance under oaks and maples, snow-capped mountain views on clear days, easiest trek conditions
Autumn: clearest skies, sharp mountain views (Gangotri range, Bandarpoonch), golden meadows, fiery red maple leaves, crisp pine-scented air, photography-friendly
Summer: hazy atmosphere with dust and pollen reduces sharpness of views; snow-capped mountains visible on clear days
Autumn: clearest skies with sharp mountain views of Gangotri range and Bandarpoonch over golden meadows; best for photography
primary_watchouts: Winter: snow raises trek difficulty by a notch; prepare well
Summer: hazy atmosphere with dust and pollen reduces view sharpness
Summer: those seeking sharp, clear mountain views (due to haze/dust/pollen)
Initial climb into the forest from Raithal is steep
Final climb to Dayara top is steep
best_for: Winter: snow experience, snowfall, snowball fights
Spring: colour contrasts of snow, greenery and flowers; rhododendron blooms
Summer: easiest trek conditions; family batches and introducing children to the Himalayas
Autumn: sharp mountain views, photography, autumn colours (golden meadows, fiery red maple leaves)
not_ideal_for: Summer: those seeking sharp, clear mountain views (due to haze/dust/pollen)

video_title: Why We Love Dayara Bugyal Trek | A Tribute by Indiahikes
